message:
ci: live-MCP uptime workflow + status badges (issue #8)

Adds .github/workflows/uptime.yml — every 15 min (and on workflow_dispatch)
the workflow probes the three production MCP endpoints with the existing
scripts/smoke-live.sh and writes a shields.io endpoint-format JSON per
target into badges/. README references the JSONs via raw.githubusercontent
so the three new status pills next to the crates.io badge flip red within
one cron tick of any target going down.

Why 15 min not 5: Cloud Run scales to zero. Hitting more aggressively
would measure cold-start latency and produce false RED on healthy
services. 15 min matches the README's CTA expectation without paying
for warm instances.

Seeded badges/ with the current state (all three green at commit time —
rulake-mcp 8 tools, rvdna-mcp 5 tools, ruqu-mcp 5 tools) so the badges
show green from first paint, not "not found" until the first cron run.

Workflow has contents:write + issues:write permissions: the first scoped
to push refreshed JSONs back to the running branch (skipped when no diff,
so we don't spam main with no-op commits), the second to optionally file
a single 'live-down' labelled issue per outage (deduped against existing
open issues so we don't refile every 15 min while down).

Closes #8.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,148 @@
+# Live-MCP uptime probes — closes issue #8.
+#
+# Hits each of the three production MCP endpoints every 15 min using
+# scripts/smoke-live.sh (the same script `scripts/smoke-all.sh` uses for
+# its production rows), then writes a shields.io endpoint-format JSON
+# per target into badges/. The README references those JSON files via
+# https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=... so the README's status badges
+# flip red within ~15 min of any target going down.
+#
+# Why 15 min, not 5: Cloud Run scales to zero. Hitting it more
+# aggressively would just measure cold-start latency and produce false
+# RED on healthy services. 15 min matches the README's CTA expectation
+# ("the wire's up") without paying for warm instances.
+#
+# Why we commit to main (not gh-pages): the JSON files are <100 B each,
+# rebuilt every 15 min, and shields.io reads them via raw.githubusercontent
+# off main — no Pages build needed. The commit message + [skip ci] tag
+# keeps these out of the ci.yml + release-* workflows' notification
+# stream.
+
+name: uptime
+
+on:
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '*/15 * * * *'
+  workflow_dispatch: {}
+
+permissions:
+  contents: write       # required to push badges/*.json back to main
+  issues: write         # required for the optional auto-issue on first failure
+
+jobs:
+  probe:
+    name: Probe live MCPs + refresh badges
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      # Probe each target independently. We capture the per-target
+      # exit code (smoke-live.sh exits 1 on any assertion failure) and
+      # synthesize the shields.io JSON from it. We deliberately do NOT
+      # `set -e` the whole step so one red probe doesn't skip the others
+      # — the badge for a healthy sibling should still flip green.
+      - name: Probe rulake-mcp.ruv.io (8 tools)
+        id: rulake
+        continue-on-error: true
+        run: |
+          set +e
+          URL=https://rulake-mcp.ruv.io/ EXPECTED_TOOLS=8 ./scripts/smoke-live.sh
+          echo "exit_code=$?" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Probe rvdna-mcp.ruv.io (5 tools)
+        id: rvdna
+        continue-on-error: true
+        run: |
+          set +e
+          URL=https://rvdna-mcp.ruv.io/ EXPECTED_TOOLS=5 ./scripts/smoke-live.sh
+          echo "exit_code=$?" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Probe ruqu-mcp.ruv.io (5 tools)
+        id: ruqu
+        continue-on-error: true
+        run: |
+          set +e
+          URL=https://ruqu-mcp.ruv.io/ EXPECTED_TOOLS=5 ./scripts/smoke-live.sh
+          echo "exit_code=$?" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Generate shields.io endpoint JSON for each target
+        run: |
+          mkdir -p badges
+          gen_badge() {
+            local label="$1"
+            local tools="$2"
+            local exit_code="$3"
+            local file="badges/${label}.json"
+            if [ "${exit_code}" = "0" ]; then
+              printf '{"schemaVersion":1,"label":"%s","message":"up · %s tools","color":"brightgreen"}\n' \
+                "${label}" "${tools}" > "${file}"
+            else
+              printf '{"schemaVersion":1,"label":"%s","message":"down","color":"red"}\n' \
+                "${label}" > "${file}"
+            fi
+            echo "wrote ${file}: $(cat ${file})"
+          }
+          gen_badge "rulake-mcp" 8 "${{ steps.rulake.outputs.exit_code }}"
+          gen_badge "rvdna-mcp"  5 "${{ steps.rvdna.outputs.exit_code }}"
+          gen_badge "ruqu-mcp"   5 "${{ steps.ruqu.outputs.exit_code }}"
+
+      # Commit only if a badge actually changed. Empty `git diff --cached`
+      # → skip the commit so we don't spam main with "no-op uptime probe"
+      # commits when every target is steady-state green.
+      - name: Commit refreshed badges to main
+        run: |
+          git config user.name  "github-actions[bot]"
+          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git add badges/
+          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
+            echo "no badge changes — skipping commit"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+          git commit -m "ci(uptime): refresh live-MCP status badges [skip ci]"
+          # Push to whatever branch the workflow is running on (main on
+          # schedule, the dispatching branch on workflow_dispatch).
+          git push origin "HEAD:${GITHUB_REF#refs/heads/}"
+
+      # Optional auto-issue on red. Only opens one issue per outage —
+      # checks for an existing open issue with the `live-down` label
+      # before filing a new one. Skip with comment if you don't want
+      # this; the badge alone is enough signal for most operators.
+      - name: Open `live-down` issue on first red
+        if: |
+          steps.rulake.outputs.exit_code != '0' ||
+          steps.rvdna.outputs.exit_code  != '0' ||
+          steps.ruqu.outputs.exit_code   != '0'
+        uses: actions/github-script@v7
+        with:
+          script: |
+            const failed = [];
+            if ('${{ steps.rulake.outputs.exit_code }}' !== '0') failed.push('rulake-mcp.ruv.io');
+            if ('${{ steps.rvdna.outputs.exit_code  }}' !== '0') failed.push('rvdna-mcp.ruv.io');
+            if ('${{ steps.ruqu.outputs.exit_code   }}' !== '0') failed.push('ruqu-mcp.ruv.io');
+
+            const { data: existing } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
+              owner: context.repo.owner,
+              repo:  context.repo.repo,
+              state: 'open',
+              labels: 'live-down',
+            });
+            if (existing.length > 0) {
+              core.info(`live-down issue #${existing[0].number} already open — not filing duplicate`);
+              return;
+            }
+
+            await github.rest.issues.create({
+              owner: context.repo.owner,
+              repo:  context.repo.repo,
+              title: `live-MCP down: ${failed.join(', ')}`,
+              labels: ['live-down'],
+              body: [
+                `Uptime workflow detected RED on the following live MCP target(s):`,
+                ``,
+                ...failed.map(t => `- \`${t}\``),
+                ``,
+                `Workflow run: ${context.serverUrl}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`,
+                ``,
+                `Auto-filed by \`.github/workflows/uptime.yml\`. Will not refile while open.`,
+              ].join('\n'),
+            });

@@ -10,6 +10,10 @@
 [![ruv.io](https://img.shields.io/badge/ruv.io-website-purple.svg)](https://ruv.io)
 [![MIT / Apache-2.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT%2FApache--2.0-blue.svg)](#license)
 
+[![rulake-mcp](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ruvnet/RuLake/main/badges/rulake-mcp.json)](https://rulake-mcp.ruv.io/)
+[![rvdna-mcp](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ruvnet/RuLake/main/badges/rvdna-mcp.json)](https://rvdna-mcp.ruv.io/)
+[![ruqu-mcp](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ruvnet/RuLake/main/badges/ruqu-mcp.json)](https://ruqu-mcp.ruv.io/)
+
 ### **Give your AI agents fast, trustworthy memory — without standing up a vector database.**
 
 ruLake is the layer between your **agents** and the **data they remember**. Plug in the storage you already have (S3, BigQuery, Snowflake, Parquet, files), expose it through one MCP tool, and every agent on every host gets the same low-latency, content-addressed view of memory.
